title: The Two Popes
logline: When Cardinal Bergoglio seeks to resign from his position, Pope Benedict XVI summons him to the Vatican for a series of conversations that will ultimately lead to a historic papal transition.
antagonistOrConflict: Internal conflict over past failures during Argentina's Dirty War, and ideological tension with Pope Benedict XVI
tone: Contemplative, intimate, and spiritually searching with moments of warmth and humor

actBreaks.description: The story shifts from the 2005 papal conclave to 2012, establishing Bergoglio's desire to resign and setting up the central dramatic encounter.

validation.ruling: Scene #31 shows Bergoglio receiving a letter from the Pope, which fundamentally shifts the story from his ordinary life in Buenos Aires to being summoned to the Vatican, creating an irreversible plot development that launches Act 2.

actBreaks.description: The conversation moves into the Sistine Chapel where deeper confessions and the exploration of Bergoglio's past during Argentina's Dirty War begins.

validation.ruling: Scene #88 shows the dramatic first meeting between Bergoglio and Ratzinger alone in the empty Sistine Chapel, where they begin their deep theological and personal discussions, marking the transition from setup to the intense personal revelations of Act 3.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a sophisticated non-linear structure that weaves between three time periods: the 2005 conclave that elected Benedict, the 2012 conversations at Castel Gandolfo and the Vatican, and extensive flashbacks to Bergoglio's formative experiences in 1950s-1980s Argentina. The structure mirrors the confessional nature of the story itself - beginning with surface-level theological disagreements and gradually peeling back layers to reveal deeper personal and moral reckonings. The Sistine Chapel becomes the sacred space where both men confront their failures and find redemption.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing deliberately slows in the middle section (scenes 46-87) as the two men engage in philosophical debates at Castel Gandolfo, building intimacy before the emotional revelations. The Argentina flashbacks are strategically placed to deepen our understanding of Bergoglio's guilt and growth, with the most intense sequences (scenes 90-112) occurring during the Sistine Chapel confession.

incitingIncident.description: News reports detail Vatican scandals including leaked documents revealing factionalism, Vatican bank malpractice, and sexual misconduct, with the Pope's own assistant Paolo Gabrielle arrested for theft.

validation.ruling: Scene #31 cannot serve as both act break and inciting incident. Scene #33 shows news reports detailing Vatican scandals including leaked documents, Vatican bank malpractice, and sexual misconduct, which shatters the status quo and forces the Pope into crisis mode, establishing the main conflict that drives the story forward.

midpoint.description: Young Bergoglio kneels in the confessional, makes the sign of the cross, and begins confession while asking the unknown priest from Parque Patricios where he's from before they speak.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. CONFESSIONAL BOX/BASILICA OF ST JOSEPH OF FLORES (1955) -

validation.ruling: Position sanity: original at 56.5%. Corrected to nearest turning-point in 35-72% window.

climax.description: Bergoglio cries at the memory while telling Ratzinger that Yorio thought him a traitor until death, then Ratzinger requests that Bergoglio hear his confession instead of Father Carmine.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. VATICAN THE CHAMBER OF TEARS (2012) - DAY

validation.ruling: Scene #126 represents the emotional and spiritual climax where Bergoglio breaks down crying about his past failures, and Ratzinger makes the pivotal decision to have Bergoglio hear his confession - the ultimate confrontation with guilt, forgiveness, and transformation that resolves the central character arcs.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLA 21 (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: A smartly-dressed boy walks through the poor Villa 21 area toward an outdoor mass celebrated by Archbishop Bergoglio, as Bergoglio's sermon about Francesco Bernadone plays in voiceover.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLA 21 - OUTDOOR MASS (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio finishes his sermon to the outdoor congregation, telling them that even glorious journeys can start with mistakes and that God will not give up on them.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY WITH ARCHES, THE VATICAN (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: Cardinal Ratzinger walks through Vatican corridors with his assistant Paolo, correctly identifying world leaders from flash cards including Lula of Brazil and Queen Margaret of Denmark.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUENOS AIRES EZEIZA AIRPORT (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio struggles through crowds toward Departures at Buenos Aires airport.

scenes.heading: INT. BUENOS AIRES EZEIZA (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: At airport security, Bergoglio instinctively raises his hands when approached by a guard, but the guard only wants a blessing, which Bergoglio provides.

scenes.heading: INT. VATICAN SECURITY ROOM (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: Ratzinger oversees Vatican security preparations for world leaders attending the papal funeral, with monitors showing Chirac, Bush, and Mandela.

scenes.heading: INT. IMPROVISED CHANGING ROOM - ST PETER’S BASILICA (2005) -
scenes.summary: Ratzinger practices his funeral sermon in front of a mirror, trying different intonations and attempting to memorize it.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST PETER'S SQUARE (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: Ratzinger delivers his sermon about Pope John Paul II's final Easter blessing with ease and authority from the pulpit in St. Peter's Square.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. PETER'S SQUARE (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: Cardinals filter across St. Peter's Square after the funeral, some giving interviews to TV crews while a journalist explains the upcoming conclave process.

scenes.heading: EXT. VATICAN PARKING LOT (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio jokes with Cardinals Turkson and Hummes about the papal election being like the World Cup, predicting the Germans will win on penalties.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM/ SISTINE CHAPEL (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: Ratzinger encounters Bergoglio in the Sistine Chapel bathroom, where Bergoglio was whistling 'Dancing Queen,' leading to an awkward exchange between the two men.

scenes.heading: INT. CURIA MEETING ROOM (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: Cardinals gather in the Curia meeting room before the conclave, with Ratzinger telling Arinze they need to protect the Pope's legacy and build strong walls around the gospel.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. PETER'S SQUARE + ARCHIVE (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: TV reporters from different countries explain that the cardinals will communicate with the outside world only through chimney smoke - white for a new Pope, black for no decision.

scenes.heading: INT. - STAIRS TO THE SISTINE CHAPEL (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: Cardinals climb the majestic stairs toward the Sistine Chapel, lined by Swiss guards.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. PETER’S SQUARE (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: The square fills with people watching on big screens and telling rosary beads as reporters announce the great doors will be locked until a new Pope is chosen.

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: A big key locks the Sistine Chapel door with a definitive sound, followed by silence as the last cardinals take their places.
scenes.turningPointNote: The conclave officially begins, sealing the cardinals inside to elect the new Pope

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: Cardinals vote in ritual choreography until the first results are announced: Ratzinger receives 47 votes, Bergoglio receives 10, shocking Bergoglio who can barely hear the rest.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bergoglio emerges as an unexpected contender for the papacy, fundamentally changing the dynamics

scenes.heading: EXT. ST PETER’S SQUARE (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: Black smoke emerges from the chimney as TV reporters and the disappointed crowd confirm no candidate achieved the required seventy-seven votes.

scenes.heading: INT. ST. MARTA (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: During the lunch break, Ratzinger lobbies cardinals about needing one unchanging eternal truth as a reference point, while Bergoglio downplays his ten votes to friends.

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: The second vote results are announced with Ratzinger receiving 65 votes and Bergoglio's support increasing to 35 votes, followed by more black smoke.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. PETER'S SQUARE (2005) - EVENING
scenes.summary: Vatican-watchers in the square sigh with disappointment at another failed vote.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUS PARKING SISTINE CHAPEL (2005) - EVENING
scenes.summary: As cardinals board mini-buses, Arinze discreetly tells Bergoglio he can bring fifteen votes if their breakfast conversation goes well, suggesting Ratzinger would have changed things by now if he could.

scenes.heading: INT. MINI BUS (2005) - EVENING
scenes.summary: In the mini-bus, Hummes whispers to Bergoglio that if conservatives are taking him seriously, he'll need to get a refund on his return flight.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOMS - ST. MARTA (2005) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Hummes finds an A4 envelope under his door and discovers identical envelopes have been placed under every cardinal's room door.

scenes.heading: INT. ST. MARTA - BREAKFAST ROOM (2005) - MORNING
scenes.summary: Hummes tells Bergoglio the envelope contains a dossier about his past, suggesting he has an enemy, possibly the government back home, as other cardinals glance at Bergoglio discretely.
scenes.turningPointNote: A damaging dossier about Bergoglio's past is distributed, threatening to derail his papal candidacy

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: Cardinals continue the voting choreography, walking up to cast their ballots which are then counted.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. PETER’S SQUARE (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: White smoke finally emerges from the chimney after initial confusion, causing huge cheers in St. Peter's Square and around the world as bells start ringing.
scenes.turningPointNote: A new Pope has been elected, ending the conclave

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM OF TEARS / VATICAN (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: The new Pope is dressed in the Room of Tears with red velvet shoes, papal vestments, white skull cap, and papal ring selected from three options each.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. PETER'S SQUARE (2005) - DAY
scenes.summary: Cardinal Estevez appears on the balcony and announces in multiple languages that they have a new Pope - Joseph Cardinal Ratzinger, who will be called Benedict XVI.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ratzinger is officially announced as the new Pope Benedict XVI

scenes.heading: INT. VATICAN (2005) - EVENING
scenes.summary: Bergoglio tells Hummes he plans to return to Buenos Aires and plan his retirement, but Hummes suggests he's too young to retire and asks what he'll do for lunch.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUENOS AIRES GALLERIA (2012)- SATURDAY EVENING
scenes.summary: Seven years later, Bergoglio banters with a stamp vendor and customer Lisabetta outside a barbershop, joking that his letters get no replies because the stamps don't work, while Lisabetta teases that people don't reply because they don't want to talk to him.

scenes.heading: INT. PRIVATE CHAPEL, VATICAN (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Pope Benedict prays alone in Latin in his private chapel, then snuffs out a candle whose smoke drifts strangely sideways instead of rising, causing him to look up and try to waft it upward.

scenes.heading: EXT. /INT (2012) - NEWS REPORT
scenes.summary: News reports detail Vatican scandals including leaked documents revealing factionalism, Vatican bank malpractice, and sexual misconduct, with the Pope's own assistant Paolo Gabrielle arrested for theft.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUENOS AIRES HIGHWAY TO AIRPORT (2012) - END OF DAY.
scenes.summary: Bergoglio looks out a bus window at a passing shanty town on his way to the airport.

scenes.heading: INT. FIUMICINO - LEONARDO DA VINCI (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio arrives at Rome airport dressed as a simple parish priest, surprising a liveried driver who was expecting someone better dressed, and declines help with his bag.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIUMICINO - LEONARDO DA VINCI (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio stares at the massive Mercedes waiting for him and insists on riding in the front passenger seat despite the driver's protests.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: In the car with motorcycle escorts, Bergoglio tells the papal driver a joke about the Pope speeding and getting pulled over, which the driver insists never happened.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROADS (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio realizes they're not going to Rome but to the Pope's summer residence, joking that maybe that's why Benedict didn't get his letters.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROADS IN LAZIALE (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: The driver tells Bergoglio that the Pope prefers bishops and cardinals to dress appropriately, so Bergoglio changes into his black cassock behind a snack truck.

scenes.heading: EXT. CASTEL GANDOLFO (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: The papal limousine arrives at the wealthy and powerful Castel Gandolfo summer retreat.

scenes.heading: INT. POPE’S OFFICE/CASTEL GANDOLFO (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Pope Benedict watches Bergoglio's arrival from his office window while his assistant Roberto takes a phone call about a report the Pope is reading, assuring the caller that Benedict is aware and will return to Rome soon.

scenes.heading: EXT. GARDEN/CASTEL GANDOLFO (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: A nun leads Bergoglio into an immaculate formal garden where he waits on a stone bench, and when she brings an umbrella saying 'in Rome, one never knows what will happen next,' he begins reading his breviary.

scenes.heading: INT. CASTEL GANDOLFO (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Benedict walks along a corridor with Roberto but is intercepted by a Vatican official with an iPad, seeming like a prisoner as he's turned aside from his intended path.

scenes.heading: EXT. CASTEL GANDOLFO - GARDEN BRIDGE (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio continues reading in the intensifying heat, checks his watch, mops his brow, unties his shoes, and decides to chat with a nearby gardener.

scenes.heading: EXT. CASTLE GANDOLFO - GARDEN BRIDGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Benedict crosses a bridge to the garden but finds only Bergoglio's suitcase on the bench, then spots him talking easily with the gardener and marvels to Roberto how comfortable Bergoglio looks.

scenes.heading: EXT. CASTLE GANDOLFO - HERB GARDEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio discusses oregano with the gardener, sharing memories of his mother's cooking, when Benedict arrives and transforms from a rattled old man into a dignified pontiff, greeting Bergoglio in Spanish.
scenes.turningPointNote: The long-awaited meeting between Benedict and Bergoglio finally begins

scenes.heading: EXT. CASTEL GANDOLFO - MAZE 1 (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Walking through a narrow maze with Bergoglio holding an umbrella over Benedict, the Pope challenges Bergoglio about giving sacraments to divorced people, and Bergoglio responds that sacraments are food for the starving, not rewards for the virtuous.

scenes.heading: EXT. CASTLE GANDOLFO, ARCH - DAY
scenes.summary: Their theological argument continues as Bergoglio quotes Mark about calling sinners and argues that mercy blows down walls, while Benedict defends the need for strong walls and boundaries.

scenes.heading: EXT. - CASTLE GANDOLFO - TEA TABLE - DAY
scenes.summary: At tea, Benedict explains his dilemma - that allowing Bergoglio to resign would look like a protest against the Church, and directly challenges Bergoglio about whether he thinks the Church is failing, leading to Bergoglio's admission that they're losing people.
scenes.turningPointNote: Benedict directly confronts Bergoglio about his resignation request and forces him to admit the Church is failing

scenes.heading: EXT. CASTLE GANDOLFO - WOODS - DAY
scenes.summary: Moving into unfamiliar wooded terrain, Benedict becomes frail and disoriented while Bergoglio takes his arm, as their argument escalates with Bergoglio criticizing the Church's focus on doctrine while ignoring planetary destruction and inequality.

scenes.heading: EXT. CASTLE GANDOLFO - BENCH IN THE WOODS
scenes.summary: Ratzinger sits on a bench in the woods while Bergoglio explains he ordered a priest stripped of ministry and put on canonical trial, dismissing confession as 'magic words' that don't heal victims.

scenes.heading: EXT. CASTEL GANDOLFO (2012) - EVENING
scenes.summary: A nun tells Bergoglio that the Pope has retired until dinner and shows him to his room, surprising him that he's staying overnight.

scenes.heading: INT. CASTEL GANDOLFO - CORRIDOR (2012) - EVENING
scenes.summary: The nun leads Bergoglio through intimidating corridors to his room and gives him a message from Buenos Aires about a football lineup, which he jokes is bad news.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM / CASTEL GANDOLFO (2012) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Bergoglio arrives for dinner to find the table set only for one, learning that the Pope eats alone in his room but has arranged for them to share the same Bavarian meal.

scenes.heading: INT. PAPAL APARTMENTS (2012) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ratzinger eats the same unappetizing soup alone in his papal apartments, filling his wine glass with Fanta while hearing distant nun-laughter from below.

scenes.heading: INT. CASTEL GANDOLFO - TV ROOM / MUSIC ROOM (2012) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Bergoglio channel-flicks looking for a football match when Ratzinger enters wearing a Bavarian vest, discussing his fitness watch and allowing Bergoglio to watch football despite not understanding the excitement himself.

scenes.heading: EXT. TANGO CLUB/ BUENOS AIRES - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Young Bergoglio dances tango passionately with his girlfriend Amalia in 1955 Buenos Aires while his voiceover describes feeling abandoned by God's silence after asking for a sign about priesthood.

scenes.heading: INT. HICKTHIER FOOD LABORATORY (1955)
scenes.summary: Young Bergoglio hands identical test results to his boss Esther at the food laboratory, revealing he hasn't actually re-done the experiment as required.

scenes.heading: EXT. FLORES BASILICA (1955) - EVENING
scenes.summary: Young Bergoglio buys yellow roses and stops outside the basilica when he hears unexpected music playing inside.

scenes.heading: INT. AMALIA’S BEDROOM (1955) - EVENING
scenes.summary: Amalia gets ready for Jorge, putting on a pretty dress and makeup while her excited mother places a flower in her hair, knowing what's planned.

scenes.heading: INT. BASILICA OF ST JOSEPH OF FLORES (1955) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Young Bergoglio enters the empty basilica where a priest waves him into the confessional, assuming that's what he came for despite his protests.

scenes.heading: INT. CONFESSIONAL BOX/BASILICA OF ST JOSEPH OF FLORES (1955) -
scenes.summary: Young Bergoglio kneels in the confessional, makes the sign of the cross, and begins confession while asking the unknown priest from Parque Patricios where he's from before they speak.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bergoglio's unexpected confession becomes the moment of his calling to priesthood

scenes.heading: EXT. CORDOBA (ARGENTINA) (1955) - DAY
scenes.summary: The confessional door opens onto a vast empty landscape where young Bergoglio walks across rocks and sand in his best clothes, stepping into the shadow of a great rock.

scenes.heading: EXT. RICOLETA (1955) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Young Bergoglio waits with roses for Amalia, who arrives with her chaperone, but they perform a choreographed breakup where she takes the flowers then walks away, looking back once.

scenes.heading: EXT. COLEGIO MAXIMO (1958) - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Bergoglio arrives at the Colegio Maximo seminary where he's warmly greeted by the novice master Fr. Jalics who welcomes him home.

scenes.heading: INT. COLEGIO MAXIMO (1958) - DAY
scenes.summary: Jalics leads Bergoglio into the seminary, asking what he's saying no to, and Bergoglio replies that his renunciation has a name while Jalics speaks about love having many names.

scenes.heading: INT. MUSIC ROOM/CASTEL GANDOLFO (2012) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ratzinger stares at Bergoglio and asks him to be brothers for the evening, then opens the piano and plays a Czech lullaby while discussing his album recorded at the same studio as The Beatles.

scenes.heading: EXT. CASTEL GANDOLFO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The sun sets over Lake Albano under a starry sky with a shooting star, but a telephone starts ringing non-stop, breaking the peaceful moment.

scenes.heading: INT. CASTEL GANDOLFO - DINING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Roberto interrupts Bergoglio's breakfast to announce the Pope has been called back to Rome on urgent business and they must leave immediately while phones ring constantly.

scenes.heading: EXT. CASTEL GANDOLFO - MORNING
scenes.summary: The gardener gives Bergoglio oregano cuttings to plant at home as Roberto hurries him to a waiting car.

scenes.heading: EXT. CASTEL GANDOLFO (2012) - HELIPAD DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio stares in amazement as the car arrives at a helipad where a helicopter waits with Ratzinger already on board.

scenes.heading: INT. PAPAL HELICOPTER (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: In the helicopter, Ratzinger asks about the oregano and Bergoglio's popularity, leading Ratzinger to observe that when he tries to be himself, people don't seem to like him very much.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE VATICAN (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: An aerial view sweeps over Vatican City and the dome of St. Peter's.

scenes.heading: EXT. HELIPORT (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: The helicopter lands and Ratzinger is immediately given a fat report by advisers and clergy, receiving a briefing about a large crisis as he climbs into a car, looking tired and heavily burdened.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. PETER'S SQUARE (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio leaves the Vatican with his bag and encounters journalists asking about recent events, but he deflects by talking about football and Argentina instead of the crisis.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS - ROME (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio glances back at the Vatican facade and walks through city crowds toward the Domus Internationalis as an ordinary man.

scenes.heading: INT. DOMUS INTERNATIONALIS (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio washes his socks while watching TV news about a Vatican bank scandal involving seized deposits and off-shore tax haven operations that forced the Pope's return from Castel Gandolfo.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPORTS BAR, ROMA (2012) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Bergoglio hurries toward a sports bar with an Argentinian flag and jerseys on the wall, recognizing it from previous trips.

scenes.heading: INT. RATZINGER’S BEDROOM - VATICAN (2012) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Benedict kneels in intense prayer in his Vatican bedroom.

scenes.heading: INT. SPORTS BAR ROMA (2012) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Bergoglio watches Argentina play Ecuador with fellow fans, leading them in prayers of thanks for a goal and asking God to help control a player's temper.

scenes.heading: INT. RATZINGER'S BEDROOM - VATICAN (2012) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ratzinger prays the Nunc Dimittis in Latin while Bergoglio's voiceover translates the prayer about letting a servant depart in peace.

scenes.heading: INT. DOMUS INTERNATIONALIS BEDROOM (2012) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Bergoglio says his own prayers, specifically praying for Ratzinger to have a quiet night and perfect end as the sun rises over Rome.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR VATICAN (2012) - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Roberto runs frantically through Vatican corridors while his phone buzzes, telling Ratzinger (being dressed in his underwear) that they can't find Bergoglio anywhere.

scenes.heading: INT. VATICAN SECURITY ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Head of Vatican Security listens to Roberto's request to find Bergoglio and gives orders to his staff.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST PETER’S SQUARE - DAY
scenes.summary: A Swiss Guard at St. Peter's Square listens to his walkie-talkie and looks around searching.

scenes.heading: EXT. VATICAN - PAOLO VI (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio walks back toward the Vatican through crowds of journalists who ask about Father Scarano's arrest, and he deflects with humor before a Swiss Guard interrupts.

scenes.heading: INT. VATICAN STAIRS (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: A young Swiss Guard leads Bergoglio up Vatican stairs, and they chat about the guard's uncomfortable but Michelangelo-designed uniform and his economics studies.

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio enters the empty Sistine Chapel and marvels at the Last Judgment when Ratzinger appears, saying he's never been there alone before and wanted to share the moment.

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL - (PART 2) - DAY
scenes.summary: Seated in the Sistine Chapel, Ratzinger tells Bergoglio he couldn't accept his resignation without speaking to him, then casually reveals his momentous decision to resign the papacy.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ratzinger's announcement of his papal resignation is the central revelation that transforms the entire story

scenes.heading: INT. CONFESSIONAL - BASILICA DE FLORES (1955) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Back in 1955, young Bergoglio completes his confession as the priest tells him to go and sin no more, but the confessional door doesn't lead to the basilica.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVADAVIA (1976) - DAY
scenes.summary: An armored car rumbles past in 1976 Argentina as people hide behind curtains and shutters, too scared to protest while soldiers barge through.

scenes.heading: EXT. ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE/ DIRTY WAR/ ARGENTINA - DAY
scenes.summary: Archive footage shows life under Argentina's military dictatorship with tanks, protests, and violence while Bergoglio's voiceover explains he became head of Jesuits during this period when 30,000 people disappeared.
scenes.turningPointNote: Introduction of the Dirty War context that will define Bergoglio's greatest moral crisis

scenes.heading: EXT. PLAZA DE MAYO (1977) - DAY
scenes.summary: Archive footage shows the Mothers of Plaza de Mayo including Esther, who speaks about thousands of suffering mothers wanting to know where their children are, alive or dead.

scenes.heading: INT. ESTHER’S HOUSE (1977) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio argues with his atheist Marxist friend Esther in her activist home, selecting books to remove while she asks how he knows they're watching her.

scenes.heading: INT. SEMINARY LIBRARY (1976) - DAY
scenes.summary: Seminary workers remove Marxist books from library shelves under Bergoglio's orders while an angry Father Jalics watches, as Bergoglio's voiceover explains his role protecting the Jesuit order.

scenes.heading: INT. BASILICA (1976) - DAY
scenes.summary: During Mass, Bergoglio gives communion but refuses the wafer to a remarried woman, instead making the sign of the cross to bless her while following church rules.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVADAVIA - CAR WORKSHOP (1976) - DAY
scenes.summary: Yorio and Jalics oversee a car repair workshop run by young people in the slums as Bergoglio's voiceover states he closed down their slum projects.

scenes.heading: INT. RIVADAVIA - CAR WORKSHOP (1976) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio arrives at the slum workshop where Jalics warmly introduces him to Luis and explains how young men learn skills and about injustice, but Bergoglio orders them to close Rivadavia down.

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: In the Sistine Chapel, Ratzinger tells Bergoglio he made a difficult, imperfect choice, and Bergoglio responds that he failed to protect his priests and people as he was supposed to.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUENOS AIRES EZEIZA (1976) - DAY
scenes.summary: Father Dourron pedals his bicycle and sees military vehicles approaching the Mission with troops positioning at both ends of the street, forcing him to hide behind a fence.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVADAVIA (1976) - DAY
scenes.summary: Soldiers pile out of trucks and surround the Mission while Jalics tries to reach it but is forced to his knees at gunpoint, and paramilitary forces take Yorio from the Mission to a truck.

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio tells Ratzinger that his former teachers Fathers Jorio and Yalics were taken away and tortured for months, and that his plea to Massera wasn't enough to save them.

scenes.heading: INT. ADMIRAL MASSERA’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio enters Admiral Massera's office to plead for the release of two priests, Fathers Jalics and Yorio, but Massera only offers to make inquiries.

scenes.heading: INT. ESMA - DAY
scenes.summary: Jalics and Yorio sit semi-naked in cells with sacks over their heads while Bergoglio's voiceover admits his words had no impact and that he did what was safe.

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio mentions the date December 18th, 1977 to Ratzinger, looking haunted.

scenes.heading: INT. HERCULES MILITARY PLANE (1977) - DAY
scenes.summary: On a military plane, Esther and other women are drugged by naval officers and then thrown one by one from the aircraft flying over the ocean to their deaths.

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio tells Ratzinger about bodies washing up on shores and questions where Christ was during the dictatorship and where he himself was.

scenes.heading: EXT. CORDOBA (MID 1980’S) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio walks alone through desolate, dusty hills while Ratzinger's voiceover reflects on feeling lost on the path.

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Ratzinger places his hand on Bergoglio's and begins defending him, revealing he knows from files that Bergoglio used his authority to save dozens of lives by hiding families in the seminary.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ratzinger shifts from listener to defender, revealing he knows about Bergoglio's heroic actions

scenes.heading: INT. COLEGIO MAXIMO OFFICE (MID 1980’S) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio surrenders his keys to the Rector as Ratzinger's voiceover describes how he was stripped of authority, cast out, and sent in shame to Cordoba.

scenes.heading: INT. SMALL CHAPEL/ CORDOBA (MID 1980’S) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio delivers a sermon to simple country folk in a chapel, comparing prayer to a TV signal that sometimes comes through clearly and sometimes doesn't.

scenes.heading: INT. SMALL CHAPEL CORDOBA (MID 1980’S) - DAY
scenes.summary: During communion in a slum church, Bergoglio gives the wafer to a divorced woman and others who confess their perceived unworthiness, showing mercy to all.

scenes.heading: EXT. SMALL CHAPEL/ CORDOBA (MID 1980’S) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio shares mate with people outside the chapel, appearing light and happy, then walks with priests, camps by a lake, and plays football with teenagers.

scenes.heading: EXT. CORDOBA (MID 1980’S) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio walks alone along a dirty road with huge mountains behind him, appearing insignificant compared to the landscape.

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Ratzinger tells Bergoglio it took years until Cardinal Quarracino noticed him at an international conference where he gave a famous speech.

scenes.heading: INT. MODERN BUILDING - BUENOS AIRES - 2000 - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio speaks to a packed international audience about a new tyranny of unfair economic structures, but is interrupted by a protestor who accuses him of being friends with tyrants.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUENOS AIRES (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: A drone shot shows Bergoglio being driven on a trike through Villa 21, with people greeting him as he passes.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLA 21 (2002), FOOD KITCHEN AND CLOTHES DISTRIBUTION
scenes.summary: Bergoglio helps out in a food kitchen and distributes clothes while his voiceover describes each act as penance for those he banished.

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Ratzinger offers to absolve Bergoglio, telling him to believe in the mercy he preaches, and formally absolves him in the name of the Trinity.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ratzinger formally absolves Bergoglio of his guilt, completing his spiritual healing

scenes.heading: INT. VATICAN - CHAMBER OF TEARS (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio asks if Ratzinger is hungry and suggests getting coffee and pizzas from a place near Via de Porta Angelica, surprising Roberto.

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Roberto crosses the Sistine Chapel as tourists enter and guards tell them no photos are allowed.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROME - VIA DE PORTA ANGELICA (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Roberto uncomfortably orders takeaway food at a bar, having never said those words before in his life.

scenes.heading: INT. VATICAN - CHAMBER OF TEARS (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: In the Chamber of Tears, Ratzinger explains the room's name and Roberto brings coffee, fanta and pizza, after which Ratzinger giggles and jokes about Jesuit food tasters.

scenes.heading: INT. SACRISTY - CHURCH, ARGENTINA (1980’S) - DAY
scenes.summary: In a vestry, Bergoglio turns to see Father Jalics enter, creating visible tension between the two men as they acknowledge each other.

scenes.heading: INT. MASS (1980’S) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio and Jalics con-celebrate mass together, with Jalics pouring water on Bergoglio's hands, and they look at each other with mutual compassion during the peace.

scenes.heading: INT. VATICAN THE CHAMBER OF TEARS (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio cries at the memory while telling Ratzinger that Yorio thought him a traitor until death, then Ratzinger requests that Bergoglio hear his confession instead of Father Carmine.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ratzinger asks Bergoglio to hear his confession, reversing their roles completely

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Ratzinger and Bergoglio cross the crowded Sistine Chapel where people surround Ratzinger asking for blessings and selfies while Bergoglio watches him enjoy the moment.

scenes.heading: EXT. VATICAN FORECOURT (2012) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio carries his bags to a car as Ratzinger tells him about St. Francis initially misunderstanding God's request to restore the church, thinking it meant bricks and mortar.

scenes.heading: INT. APOSTOLIC PALACE (2013) - DAY
scenes.summary: During a routine canonization service, Ratzinger announces his resignation to sleepy bishops and cardinals, delivering the news in Latin.
scenes.turningPointNote: Pope Benedict announces his historic resignation

scenes.heading: INT. CATHEDRAL KITCHEN (2013) - DAY
scenes.summary: Bergoglio watches the resignation announcement on TV in the kitchen with Lisabetta, laughing that Ratzinger delivered difficult news in Latin so only a few cardinals would understand.

scenes.heading: EXT. VATICAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Cardinals arrive for the Conclave with journalists taking photographs while Bergoglio gets off a bus and walks in unnoticed by the press.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. PETER'S SQUARE - DAY
scenes.summary: The chimney at St. Peter's Square sits smokeless.

scenes.heading: INT. SISTINE CHAPEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Cardinals cast their votes while a reluctant-looking Bergoglio sits weighed down and fearful as the Third Scrutineer repeatedly calls out his name from the ballots.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bergoglio is elected Pope despite his reluctance

scenes.heading: EXT. ST PETER’S SQUARE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: White smoke spills from the chimney as crowds gather in the rainy square, and the Protodeacon prepares to announce the new Pope to the roaring crowd in multiple languages.

scenes.heading: INT. VATICAN - CHAMBER OF TEARS (2013) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the Chamber of Tears, the newly elected Pope Francis chooses to wear his own simple clothes and black shoes instead of the traditional papal robes and red shoes.

scenes.heading: EXT. BALCONY / VATICAN (2013) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Pope Francis waves to the crowds from the balcony and greets them with "Buona Sera."

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM NEXT TO BALCONY / VATICAN (2013) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Camerlengo reacts with surprise at the Pope's informal greeting, whispering "Buona Sera?" to a nun.

scenes.heading: EXT. BALCONY / VATICAN (2013) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Pope Francis addresses the crowd in Italian, explaining that his brother Cardinals went to the ends of the Earth to find a bishop for Rome, and asks them to pray for him before he blesses them.

scenes.heading: INT. TV ROOM/ MATER ECCLESIAE/ VATICAN (2013) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Benedict watches the ceremony on TV in his room, recognizing it as a personal message for him.

scenes.heading: EXT. VATICAN BALCONY (2013) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Pope Francis continues addressing the crowds about beginning a journey of brotherhood and love together, breaking tradition by asking them to pray for him first.

scenes.heading: INT. FLAT IN BUENOS AIRES (2013) - DAY
scenes.summary: Lisabetta watches the papal ceremony on TV and starts crying, telling her husband she feels bad for the new Pope because he'll never have his life back or come home again.

scenes.heading: EXT. - VILLA 21 (2013) - DAY
scenes.summary: People gather around Padre Pepe in Villa 21 to watch the papal ceremony on TV.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM NEXT TO THE BALCONY. / VATICAN (2013) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The new Pope Francis asks where the other cardinals are and insists on traveling back with them in the mini-van rather than alone in a limousine.

scenes.heading: INT. ST. MARTA (2013) - DAY
scenes.summary: Pope Francis makes a phone call to book a flight, navigating through automated voice prompts in the scene that opened the film.

scenes.heading: INT. ST. MARTA CORRIDOR (2013) - DAY
scenes.summary: Pope Francis finds a Swiss Guard outside his bedroom door and asks for help booking a flight online to Lampedusa, with the guard offering to help using his cell phone.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAMPEDUSA 8TH JULY 2013 - DAY
scenes.summary: Pope Francis preaches and teaches among refugees on Lampedusa, brings refugees to the Vatican, and shows them the Sistine Chapel while having lunch with them.

scenes.heading: INT. SANTA MARTA (2014) - DAY
scenes.summary: Pope Francis spots media-lunas in the dining room and asks to take them all to bring to Brother Benedict.

scenes.heading: EXT. MATER ECCLESIAE / VATICAN (2014) - DAY
scenes.summary: An establishing shot shows the former convent in the Vatican where Benedict now lives.

scenes.heading: INT. MATER ECCLESIAE / VATICAN (2014) - DAY
scenes.summary: Pope Francis visits Benedict in his study, wearing a San Lorenzo football scarf and bringing media-lunas, while Benedict works on a theological book about mercy.

scenes.heading: EXT. VATICAN GARDEN / GROTTO OF OUR LADY OF LOURDES (2014) -
scenes.summary: The two Popes walk together in the Vatican gardens discussing the pressures of papal office, with Francis saying he cannot relax until he knows an unspecified outcome.

scenes.heading: INT. TV ROOM/ MATER ECCLESIAE/ VATICAN (2014) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The two popes watch the World Cup Final between Germany and Argentina while eating pizza and drinking beer, singing their national anthems and reacting to the scoreless game, with Bergoglio appearing pleased when German player Schweinsteiger gets injured.

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
